pages.raw_text:
tect it!


Eight hours, beneath tropical
sun, is sufficient toll for any man,
be he white, black, red, brown or
yellow, Eight hours a day is con-
ducive to better work, better health
and more complete satisfaction all
around.


May President Gompers of the
American Federation of Labor,
backed by the moral support of
every intelligent workingman in the
United States, protest LONG and
LOUDLY, until President Roose-
welt reconsiders his decision in this
matter!


Even before the brotherhood of
unionism, comes the brotherhood Of
man! Humanity's cry has a far,
far reach; it vibrates with a deep
and bitter intonation!


In aiding their less fortunate
brethren, united labor adds
strength, dignity, tolerance and
compassion to it's cause!


True unionism-to WIN OUT
must appeal to a mans morals and
a mans sanity-not to his UNREA-
SON!


When the test" comes, union
men must prove themselves broth
ers to all men.


Humanity first! Organized labor
next!


Then must follow, as MONDAY
follows dawn, progress, comfort,
competency.
